title: Lanes & Turns
description: Choosing lanes, turning, changing lanes, and roundabouts.

scenarios.prompt: You're about to turn left. Which lane should you be in?

feedback.correct: Yes — move into the left lane before a left turn.
feedback.right: The right lane is for right turns and going straight. Use the left lane to turn left. Try again.
feedback.any: Get into the correct lane early — left lane for a left turn. Try again.
feedback.default: Use the left lane to turn left. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You want to change lanes. What should you do first?

choices.label: Signal, then check your mirror and blind spot

feedback.correct: Yes — signal first, then check your mirrors and look over your shoulder before moving.
feedback.quick: Always signal and check your blind spot before moving over. Try again.
feedback.speed: Don't force it — signal, check, then move when there's a safe gap. Try again.
feedback.movefirst: Signal and check first, then move — not the other way around. Try again.
feedback.default: Signal and check your blind spot first. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Which way do you drive around a roundabout?

choices.label: Enter to the right and go around left (counter-clockwise)

feedback.correct: Yes — you always go counter-clockwise, keeping to the right as you enter.
feedback.left: In the US you keep right and travel counter-clockwise. Try again.
feedback.straight: You drive around the circle, not across it. Try again.
feedback.clockwise: In the US you go counter-clockwise, not clockwise. Try again.
feedback.default: Go around to the right, counter-clockwise. Try again.
